question: Which issue makes it critical to understand loading and unloading procedures and teach them to students on your bus?

answers.text: Younger students will not pay attention to what is happening
More students are killed during loading and unloading than riding on a bus
answers.explanation: Safe loading and unloading are critical because more students are killed during those times than are killed while riding on the bus. Keeping students safe is the highest priority.

answers.text: To make things as efficient as possible to stay on schedule

explanation: Safe loading and unloading are critical because more students are killed during those times than are killed while riding on the bus. Keeping students safe is the highest priority.

question: What is a passive highway rail crossing?

answers.text: This type of crossing does not have any type of traffic control device.
answers.explanation: A passive crossing does not have any traffic control device. Regardless of warning indicators at a railroad crossing, a passenger bus needs to always stop at these crossings.

answers.text: This type of crossing does have a traffic control device.
This type of crossing has a lane to pass slow moving vehicles.

explanation: A passive crossing does not have any traffic control device. Regardless of warning indicators at a railroad crossing, a passenger bus needs to always stop at these crossings.

question: The most dangerous time during the school bus ride is:

answers.text: When loading and unloading students.
answers.explanation: Unattentive drivers approaching the stopped school bus are the biggest danger to children as they load and unload. Be sure that traffic has completely stopped before telling a student to unload. More children are killed during loading/unloading than at any other time.

answers.text: When the students become unruly on the bus.
Crossing railway crossing.

explanation: Unattentive drivers approaching the stopped school bus are the biggest danger to children as they load and unload. Be sure that traffic has completely stopped before telling a student to unload. More children are killed during loading/unloading than at any other time.

question: What is the appropriate action when a railroad crossing gate begins to come down after you have started to cross the tracks?

answers.text: Finish crossing and clearing the tracks
answers.explanation: Once you've committed to crossing, quickly get across and clear the tracks. Do not stop until you have cleared the crossing area and do not press the clutch to shift a manual transmission vehicle. The gate can be replaced later. Contact dispatch regarding the incident.

answers.text: Stop until the gate returns to the upright position, then continue to cross.
Stop and back up off the tracks and return to the previous waiting position.

explanation: Once you've committed to crossing, quickly get across and clear the tracks. Do not stop until you have cleared the crossing area and do not press the clutch to shift a manual transmission vehicle. The gate can be replaced later. Contact dispatch regarding the incident.

question: This type of crossing has a traffic control device installed at the crossing to regulate traffic.

answers.text: Active Crossings
answers.explanation: An active crossing will have some sort of traffic control device beyond just a stop sign and/or crossing sign.

answers.text: Passive Crossings
Flashing Crossings

explanation: An active crossing will have some sort of traffic control device beyond just a stop sign and/or crossing sign.

question: When approaching a bus stop, you should:

answers.text: Pay close attention to the children, ignore pedestrians, and traffic.
Approach cautiously, at a slow rate of speed.
answers.explanation: Approach a bus stop slowly. Begin slowing down early, usually when you activate the amber lights.

answers.text: Approach cautiously, at a constant speed.

explanation: Approach a bus stop slowly. Begin slowing down early, usually when you activate the amber lights.

question: What mirrors are used to monitor traffic and to check clearances and students on the sides and to the rear of the bus?

answers.text: Outside left and right-side flat mirrors.
answers.explanation: The left and right flat mirrors allow visibility for monitoring clearances and students on the sides and to the rear of the bus.

answers.text: Outside left and right-side cross view mirrors.
Outside left and right-side Convex mirrors.

explanation: The left and right flat mirrors allow visibility for monitoring clearances and students on the sides and to the rear of the bus.

question: What should you be able to see if the outside convex mirrors are adjusted properly?

answers.text: Front of the rear tires touching the ground, and the entire side of the bus up to the mirror mounts.
The entire side of the bus up to the mirror mounts, front of the rear tires touching the ground, and at least one traffic lane on either side of the bus.
answers.explanation: Assuming that the convex mirrors are adjusted properly a driver should have visibility of 1 lane of traffic on either side of the bus, the front of the rear tires contacting the roadway, and the entire length of the side of the bus up to the mirror mounting.

answers.text: At least one traffic lane on either side of the bus, and the entire side of the bus up to the mirror mounts.

explanation: Assuming that the convex mirrors are adjusted properly a driver should have visibility of 1 lane of traffic on either side of the bus, the front of the rear tires contacting the roadway, and the entire length of the side of the bus up to the mirror mounting.

question: Which mnemonic memory aid can help with managing students with disabilities?

answers.text: START
answers.explanation: The mnemonic START stands for Special, Treat equally, Attention, Restraints, different Techniques and can help a bus driver remember the steps to managing students with disabilities.

answers.text: STOP
HELPING

explanation: The mnemonic START stands for Special, Treat equally, Attention, Restraints, different Techniques and can help a bus driver remember the steps to managing students with disabilities.

question: Which of the following best describes the danger zone of a school bus?

answers.text: The area behind the bus while picking up students.
The area anywhere outside of the bus where children are in the most danger of being hit, either by another vehicle or their own bus.
answers.explanation: A danger zone around a school bus will include any blind spots where the bus driver cannot see children and any area where a child may be struck by traffic.

answers.text: The area on the right side of the bus not visible by any of your mirrors.

explanation: A danger zone around a school bus will include any blind spots where the bus driver cannot see children and any area where a child may be struck by traffic.

question: Which inspections must be made of the bus entry door?

answers.text: Emergency warning indicators operate when the door opens.
answers.explanation: An inspection of the entry door includes ensuring that the handrails are secure, any step lights operate, and that the door is free of damage and opens/closes security from inside the bus.

answers.text: The door is sealed from any wind noise or water leaks.
The door cannot be opened from the outside.

explanation: An inspection of the entry door includes ensuring that the handrails are secure, any step lights operate, and that the door is free of damage and opens/closes security from inside the bus.

question: What action should be taken if a bus stalls on the railroad tracks of a crossing and will not quickly restart?

answers.text: Immediately evacuate everyone out of the rear emergency exit and assemble the students about 50 feet behind the bus.
Immediately evacuate the bus and relocate students to an area away from the bus and tracks toward the direction of the expected train.
answers.explanation: Evacuate the students immediately. In the event of a train to bus collision, the destruction of the bus can cause a danger. Although it may be difficult to know the direction of a suspected train, move the students away from the bus and tracks at a 45-degree angle toward the suspected direction of the train.

answers.text: Immediately call dispatch while trying to restart the bus and request assistance.

explanation: Evacuate the students immediately. In the event of a train to bus collision, the destruction of the bus can cause a danger. Although it may be difficult to know the direction of a suspected train, move the students away from the bus and tracks at a 45-degree angle toward the suspected direction of the train.

question: Since July 1st, 1993, California requires that every bus have:

answers.text: four red flashing lights on the front and back
a total of six flashing lights on the bus
a total of eight flashing lights, two red on the front and back and two amber on the front and back
answers.explanation: Each school bus produced since July 1st of 1993 for California is required to have four flashing red lights and four flashing amber lights, two of each on the front and rear of the bus.

explanation: Each school bus produced since July 1st of 1993 for California is required to have four flashing red lights and four flashing amber lights, two of each on the front and rear of the bus.

question: Which of the following statements about loading and unloading students is true?

answers.text: Students should be told not to use the handrails when exiting the bus due to the possibility of causing a clothing hang up.
Students should be told to pick up any dropped objects as long as it is not under the bus.
Students should be told to leave any dropped object where it is, and to move to a point of safety out of the danger zone, and then attempt to get the driver's attention to retrieve the object.
answers.explanation: A student should understand that if they drop something while crossing the road, they should leave the item and get across the road as soon as possible. After they are safe, the student can signal the driver for permission to retrieve the item if it is safe to do so.

explanation: A student should understand that if they drop something while crossing the road, they should leave the item and get across the road as soon as possible. After they are safe, the student can signal the driver for permission to retrieve the item if it is safe to do so.

question: Depending on the dimensions of the school bus, the front danger zone may extend up to ______ feet ahead of the front bumper.

answers.explanation: The first 12 feet ahead of the front bumper is considered the most dangerous (fig 10-1 of the handbook). However, the danger zone may extend up to 30 feet ahead of the front bumper depending on the bus dimensions.

explanation: The first 12 feet ahead of the front bumper is considered the most dangerous (fig 10-1 of the handbook). However, the danger zone may extend up to 30 feet ahead of the front bumper depending on the bus dimensions.

question: The alarm is sounding at a railroad crossing that does not have a gate/arm. You have stopped the bus and looked/listened for several minutes and cannot see or hear an approaching train. What action should you take?

answers.text: Suspect a malfunctioning alarm and quickly cross since you cannot hear or see an approaching train.
Perform a U-turn and cross the tracks at a different location.
Call the school dispatcher and request instructions.
answers.explanation: A school bus driver is required to stop, look, and listen at all railroad crossings whether they have a simple crossbuck sign or they have an alarm system with a crossing gate. It can be extremely difficult to know when a gate or alarm is malfunctioning, but if you ever have any doubt, always call dispatch for instructions before proceeding.

explanation: A school bus driver is required to stop, look, and listen at all railroad crossings whether they have a simple crossbuck sign or they have an alarm system with a crossing gate. It can be extremely difficult to know when a gate or alarm is malfunctioning, but if you ever have any doubt, always call dispatch for instructions before proceeding.

question: When stopping on a railroad crossing, the driver must stop no closer than:

answers.text: 10 feet
20 feet
15 feet
answers.explanation: Regardless of warning indicators at a railroad crossing, a passenger bus needs to always stop between 15 and 50 feet from the crossing then look and listen for an approaching train.

explanation: Regardless of warning indicators at a railroad crossing, a passenger bus needs to always stop between 15 and 50 feet from the crossing then look and listen for an approaching train.

question: Which strategy is recommended for managing incidents of bullying or harassment on the bus?

answers.text: Document each incident and report them along with any allegations.
answers.explanation: You cannot fairly take action against "potential" bullies or try to identify those that you think may be victims. Document all incidents of actual bullying or harassment and report them. Also, report any allegations of bullying that the school may investigate themselves.

answers.text: Have potential victims of bullying sit in the back of the bus.
Have potential bullies it in seats at the front of the bus where you can monitor them.

explanation: You cannot fairly take action against "potential" bullies or try to identify those that you think may be victims. Document all incidents of actual bullying or harassment and report them. Also, report any allegations of bullying that the school may investigate themselves.

question: Which of the following must be included in the pre-trip school bus inspection?

answers.text: Ensure that emergency exit doors close securely and operate smoothly from the inside.
answers.explanation: Inspection of the emergency exits requires at least one to be undamaged, fully functional, and open/close from the inside. The door should remain secure during travel and any warning indicators operate when the door is opened.

answers.text: Spray the hinge of each door with the appropriate lubricant.
Check rear axle fluid level.

explanation: Inspection of the emergency exits requires at least one to be undamaged, fully functional, and open/close from the inside. The door should remain secure during travel and any warning indicators operate when the door is opened.

question: In flat mirrors you should be able to see:

answers.text: Pedestrians on the sidewalk on both sides of the bus.
200 feet behind the bus.
answers.explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet behind the bus, which is about four bus lengths.

answers.text: Full side of the bus and rear of bus.

explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet behind the bus, which is about four bus lengths.

question: What mirrors are used to see the danger zone?

answers.text: Outside left and right-side flat mirrors.
Outside left and right-side cross view mirrors.
answers.explanation: The left and right-side cross-view mirrors are designed to allow the bus driver to monitor the "danger zones" where the driver cannot directly see children. One danger zone is the area ahead of the front bumper, and the other two danger zones are the left and right sides of the bus.

answers.text: Outside left and right-side Convex mirrors.

explanation: The left and right-side cross-view mirrors are designed to allow the bus driver to monitor the "danger zones" where the driver cannot directly see children. One danger zone is the area ahead of the front bumper, and the other two danger zones are the left and right sides of the bus.

question: What is included in the inspection of an emergency exit door on the school bus?

answers.text: The door closes securely from the inside and any exit warning device is working.
answers.explanation: Inspection of the emergency exits states that at least one emergency exit must be undamaged, operate properly, and opens/closes from inside. Any exit warning devices must also be working. The inspector should record the condition of any other emergency exit.

answers.text: Replacing any missing rubber sealant between the door and bus.
Checking the operation of the window of the emergency door.

explanation: Inspection of the emergency exits states that at least one emergency exit must be undamaged, operate properly, and opens/closes from inside. Any exit warning devices must also be working. The inspector should record the condition of any other emergency exit.

question: Where should students be instructed to assemble during unloading from the bus?

answers.text: 10 feet from the left side of the bus
5 feet to the right side of the bus
10 to 15 feet from the right side of the bus
answers.explanation: All students will unload to the right side of the bus first. They should assemble 10 to 15 feet away within view of the driver so they can be counted. Once given the signal, students that need to cross the road may do so.

explanation: All students will unload to the right side of the bus first. They should assemble 10 to 15 feet away within view of the driver so they can be counted. Once given the signal, students that need to cross the road may do so.

question: In California, what is the minimum distance that the front bumper should be from the students when the bus stops?

answers.text: 5 feet
10 feet
answers.explanation: When stopping to load students, the front bumper should be at least 10 feet from the location of the students.

answers.text: 20 feet

explanation: When stopping to load students, the front bumper should be at least 10 feet from the location of the students.

question: What is the maximum speed limit for a school bus while transporting at least 1 student in California?

answers.text: 50 mph
55 mph
answers.explanation: California has a maximum speed limit of 55 mph for school buses when they are transporting at least one student.

answers.text: 60 mph

explanation: California has a maximum speed limit of 55 mph for school buses when they are transporting at least one student.

question: If the bus is on fire what is the first thing the driver should do?

answers.text: Grab the fire extinguisher and attempt to put out the fire.
Conduct an investigation.
Evacuate the bus.
answers.explanation: In the event of a fire on the bus, immediately evacuate the bus.

explanation: In the event of a fire on the bus, immediately evacuate the bus.

question: What is a symptom of hydroplaning?

answers.text: Seeing engine RPMs decreases without taking your foot off the accelerator.
A drifting sensation at the front of the vehicle accompanied by lack of control when turning the steering wheel.
answers.explanation: Hydroplaning occurs more often at the front wheels of a bus because they are single wheels on each side. This will result in a drifting sensation at the front of the bus or a noticeable lack of control when turning the steering wheel. If the drive wheels hydroplane, you can see the engine RPM increase or hear the engine rev up as the drive wheels start to spin.

answers.text: Hearing a thudding or flapping sound from a wheel well.

explanation: Hydroplaning occurs more often at the front wheels of a bus because they are single wheels on each side. This will result in a drifting sensation at the front of the bus or a noticeable lack of control when turning the steering wheel. If the drive wheels hydroplane, you can see the engine RPM increase or hear the engine rev up as the drive wheels start to spin.

question: The blind spot behind a school bus is usually 50 to 150 feet. However, depending on bus dimensions the blind spot may be as far as:

answers.text: 250 feet
400 feet
answers.explanation: Typically, the rear blind spot behind the bus is 50 to 150 feet. However, the blind spot may extend up to 400 feet depending on dimensions.

answers.text: 500 feet

explanation: Typically, the rear blind spot behind the bus is 50 to 150 feet. However, the blind spot may extend up to 400 feet depending on dimensions.

question: Which of the following is true of a vehicle with an antilock braking system?

answers.text: The antilock brake malfunction indicator will be red.
ABS does not affect braking under normal (non-emergency) braking conditions.
answers.explanation: Although the ABS module is always monitoring vehicle conditions, it only activates during emergency braking situations. A driver should apply as much brake pressure as they deem necessary in a situation to stop on time and stay in control of the bus. If braking is hard enough to activate the ABS, it will be felt in the brake pedal as a pulsing sensation as the ABS module cycles brake pressure off and on to the appropriate wheel(s).

answers.text: The vehicle will have a shorter braking distance.

explanation: Although the ABS module is always monitoring vehicle conditions, it only activates during emergency braking situations. A driver should apply as much brake pressure as they deem necessary in a situation to stop on time and stay in control of the bus. If braking is hard enough to activate the ABS, it will be felt in the brake pedal as a pulsing sensation as the ABS module cycles brake pressure off and on to the appropriate wheel(s).

question: Which of the following best defines an active railroad crossing?

answers.text: A crossing that only has a crossbuck sign and/or pavement markings.
A crossing that has an alarm, lights, and/or crossing gate.
answers.explanation: An active crossing is one that has a type of traffic control device, such as a crossing gate, flashing lights, and/or warning alarm. Any crossing lacking a traffic control device is a passive crossing. ALL crossings require a driver to stop, look, and listen regardless of being active or passive.

answers.text: A crossing that requires you to actively stop, look, and listen for an approaching train.

explanation: An active crossing is one that has a type of traffic control device, such as a crossing gate, flashing lights, and/or warning alarm. Any crossing lacking a traffic control device is a passive crossing. ALL crossings require a driver to stop, look, and listen regardless of being active or passive.

question: Why are school buses the safest choice of transportation to school as compared to a passenger car?

answers.text: Trained professional drivers.
answers.explanation: Per the NHTSA, school buses are the safest form of transportation to and from school due to the numerous safety features of the bus, the safety procedures for loading and unloading the bus, and the experience of professionally trained bus drivers.

answers.text: The yellow paint scheme.
The public will always stop when they see a school bus.

explanation: Per the NHTSA, school buses are the safest form of transportation to and from school due to the numerous safety features of the bus, the safety procedures for loading and unloading the bus, and the experience of professionally trained bus drivers.

question: What is the best action to take if you are driving in heavy rain and feel the bus start to hydroplane?

answers.text: Hit the brakes like an emergency stop.
Straighten out the steering wheel and accelerate.
Let off the accelerator pedal until you regain control.
answers.explanation: The first reaction should be to let off the accelerator and the corresponding slowdown should stop the hydroplaning. If the condition is not immediately corrected, you can press in the clutch on a manual transmission or shift into neutral on an automatic transmission to remove all power to the wheels and slow them quicker. This will also cause all four wheels to start rolling at the same speed if one or more had been slipping.

explanation: The first reaction should be to let off the accelerator and the corresponding slowdown should stop the hydroplaning. If the condition is not immediately corrected, you can press in the clutch on a manual transmission or shift into neutral on an automatic transmission to remove all power to the wheels and slow them quicker. This will also cause all four wheels to start rolling at the same speed if one or more had been slipping.

question: The driver who misses a student's unloading stop should

answers.text: Discharge the child on the next stop.
Back up to the missed stop.
Follow the local procedures.
answers.explanation: If a driver misses a stop the driver should follow local procedures and school protocol. Do not back up the bus on the road.

explanation: If a driver misses a stop the driver should follow local procedures and school protocol. Do not back up the bus on the road.

question: What should you be able to see if the outside flat mirrors are adjusted properly?

answers.text: 200 feet or 4 bus lengths behind the bus. Along the sides of the bus. The rear tires touching the ground.
answers.explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet, which is about four bus lengths, visibility along the sides of the bus, and the rear tires in contact with the roadway.

answers.text: 300 feet or 4 bus lengths behind the bus. Along the sides of the bus. The rear tires touching the ground.
100 feet or 3 bus lengths behind the bus. Along the sides of the bus. The rear tires touching the ground.

explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet, which is about four bus lengths, visibility along the sides of the bus, and the rear tires in contact with the roadway.

question: Which of the following will result in hydroplaning?

answers.text: Driving through water or heavy rains.
answers.explanation: Hydroplaning occurs when tire treads cannot displace water fast enough when driving through water or heavy rain. If you experience hydroplaning, slow down until it stops, and do not speed up until conditions improve.

answers.text: Driving on ice when the temperature is below freezing.
Driving on bleeding tar on a hot day.

explanation: Hydroplaning occurs when tire treads cannot displace water fast enough when driving through water or heavy rain. If you experience hydroplaning, slow down until it stops, and do not speed up until conditions improve.

question: Regarding school officials, approximately what percentage of parents will only ever meet the school bus driver and no other representatives?

answers.text: Less than 30%
About 60%
Up to 85%
answers.explanation: Up to 85% of parents will only ever meet the bus driver and no other representative of the school system. Understand that you are representing the school and be professional and courteous to parents and members of the community.

explanation: Up to 85% of parents will only ever meet the bus driver and no other representative of the school system. Understand that you are representing the school and be professional and courteous to parents and members of the community.

question: How many flashing warning lights are found on each California school bus to help control traffic at a bus stop?

answers.explanation: There are a total of eight flashing lights on a school bus to control traffic at a bus stop. Four red and four amber.

explanation: There are a total of eight flashing lights on a school bus to control traffic at a bus stop. Four red and four amber.

question: You are loading students along the route. When should you activate your alternating flashing amber warning lamps?

answers.text: These lights are always flashing when the bus is running.
If the school bus is so equipped, activate alternating flashing amber warning lamps before the school bus stop in accordance with state law.
answers.explanation: When equipped, the flashing amber lights perform a similar function to the yellow light of a traffic signal letting approaching drivers know that the light will soon turn red. Activate the flashing amber lights prior to making a complete stop, approximately 200 feet or 5 to 10 seconds before the bus stop, to signal approaching drivers that they should prepare to stop.

answers.text: Never activate these lights unless there is an emergency.

explanation: When equipped, the flashing amber lights perform a similar function to the yellow light of a traffic signal letting approaching drivers know that the light will soon turn red. Activate the flashing amber lights prior to making a complete stop, approximately 200 feet or 5 to 10 seconds before the bus stop, to signal approaching drivers that they should prepare to stop.

question: How should expected gang members be managed if you are approached on your route?

answers.text: Ignore and avoid them. Refuse to speak to them.
Be respectful and try to speak one-on-one away from others.
answers.explanation: If you must speak with a gang member, be respectful and try to speak one-on-one with them away from the students. If they are threatening or violating rules, contact dispatch and report the incident.

answers.text: Take initiative, demand that they leave and stare them down until they go.

explanation: If you must speak with a gang member, be respectful and try to speak one-on-one with them away from the students. If they are threatening or violating rules, contact dispatch and report the incident.

question: Where should you stop a bus when approaching a railroad crossing that lacks a stop line?

answers.text: 5 to 35 feet from the nearest rail of the crossing
15 to 30 feet from the nearest rail of the crossing
15 to 50 feet from the nearest rail of the crossing
answers.explanation: A bus is required to stop between 15 and 50 feet from the nearest rail of the crossing to allow adequate clearance when the train passes.

explanation: A bus is required to stop between 15 and 50 feet from the nearest rail of the crossing to allow adequate clearance when the train passes.

question: When preparing to load students, what should be the last step before giving the signal to approach the bus and opening the door?

answers.text: Extending the STOP arm
Turning on the red-flashing lights
Checking that all approaching traffic has stopped.
answers.explanation: Always check that traffic has stopped, and that nothing is entering the danger zones prior to opening the entrance door.

explanation: Always check that traffic has stopped, and that nothing is entering the danger zones prior to opening the entrance door.

question: Which statement is true of CB radio use during an evacuation emergency?

answers.text: Hang the CB mouthpiece out the driver's window so you can use it while outside the bus.
answers.explanation: When time allows, radio dispatch and inform them of your location, condition, and what assistance you require. Hang the CB mouthpiece outside the driver's window so that it can be available while you are outside of the bus.

answers.text: Turn the CB off as you secure the bus upon exit.
Switch the CB to an emergency channel before evacuating.

explanation: When time allows, radio dispatch and inform them of your location, condition, and what assistance you require. Hang the CB mouthpiece outside the driver's window so that it can be available while you are outside of the bus.

question: When stopping a bus to pick up students, your first step should be to:

answers.text: Activate the flashing red lights and extend the STOP sign arm
answers.explanation: With the amber lights already flashing as you approach the stop, come to a complete stop and then activate the red lights and extend the STOP arm. Place the transmission into Park and then wait for all approaching traffic to stop.

answers.text: Activate the flashing yellow/amber lights for 15 seconds
Extend the STOP sign arm and wait for all traffic to stop.

explanation: With the amber lights already flashing as you approach the stop, come to a complete stop and then activate the red lights and extend the STOP arm. Place the transmission into Park and then wait for all approaching traffic to stop.

question: Which of the following people are allowed to remove or suspend a student from riding the bus?

answers.text: The school administrator or the police.
answers.explanation: A school administrator or police officer is the only authority that may remove a student from the bus. As a driver, only unload a student at the school or their designated bus stop.

answers.text: The school principal
The school bus driver

explanation: A school administrator or police officer is the only authority that may remove a student from the bus. As a driver, only unload a student at the school or their designated bus stop.

question: What protocol should be followed if you cannot account for all students while loading the bus at a stop?

answers.text: Ask the students where the other student went.
Search for the student under and around the outside of the bus
answers.explanation: Count the number of students prior to loading and then count again once they are on the bus. If a student is unaccounted for, secure the bus and take the key with you. Search for the missing student under and around the outside of the bus.

answers.text: Leave the engine running, set the parking brake, and activate the four-way flashers before exiting the bus.

explanation: Count the number of students prior to loading and then count again once they are on the bus. If a student is unaccounted for, secure the bus and take the key with you. Search for the missing student under and around the outside of the bus.

question: For safety purposes, students should be taught to:

answers.text: Run across the road as quickly as possible when loading or unloading.
Approach the bus as soon as they see it has completely stopped.
Leave a dropped item on the road and finish crossing, then get your attention for permission to go back to get the item.
answers.explanation: Students should leave dropped items when crossing the road and clear the danger zone. Then, they can look to you for the signal that it is ok for them to retrieve the item before going back to pick it up.

explanation: Students should leave dropped items when crossing the road and clear the danger zone. Then, they can look to you for the signal that it is ok for them to retrieve the item before going back to pick it up.

question: Which of the following should you NOT do when driving in high winds?

answers.text: Speed up to get away from the wind as quickly as possible.
answers.explanation: Due to the size of a school bus, high winds can cause driveability issues. Keep both hands on the steering wheel and keep a firm grip to retain control during buffeting and gusts. When winds are excessive it may be necessary to slow the bus or even stop and wait for conditions to improve. If you have any doubt, contact dispatch for instructions.

answers.text: You should slow down to lessen the effect of the wind of pull off the roadway and wait.
Contact your dispatcher to get more information about what to do.

explanation: Due to the size of a school bus, high winds can cause driveability issues. Keep both hands on the steering wheel and keep a firm grip to retain control during buffeting and gusts. When winds are excessive it may be necessary to slow the bus or even stop and wait for conditions to improve. If you have any doubt, contact dispatch for instructions.

question: How should you use your brakes if your vehicle is equipped with anti-lock brakes?

answers.text: Brake the same way, regardless of whether you have ABS on the bus.
answers.explanation: The presence of an ABS should have no effect on how the driver brakes to slow the vehicle.

answers.text: Pump the brakes evenly.
Brake earlier that you normally would.

explanation: The presence of an ABS should have no effect on how the driver brakes to slow the vehicle.

question: Bus (CMV) drivers may be cited and have their license suspended if they stop on railroad tracks for failing to have enough room for the vehicle on the other side of the tracks. Ensuring that there is enough room is called containment, which is:

answers.text: at least the length of the bus plus an additional 15 feet.
answers.explanation: Regarding a railroad crossing, containment refers to having enough room to contain the vehicle once you have crossed the tracks. This area will be at least the length of the bus plus an additional 15 feet. Stopping a bus (or any CMV) on the tracks because you did not allow enough containment room can result in a citation and CDL suspension for 60 days.

answers.text: at least the length of the bus plus an additional 10 feet.
at least the length of the bus plus an additional 5 feet.

explanation: Regarding a railroad crossing, containment refers to having enough room to contain the vehicle once you have crossed the tracks. This area will be at least the length of the bus plus an additional 15 feet. Stopping a bus (or any CMV) on the tracks because you did not allow enough containment room can result in a citation and CDL suspension for 60 days.

question: Which of the following is the recommended procedure when unloading students?

answers.text: Tell students to remain seated until they are told to exit.
answers.explanation: All students should remain seated until the bus has come to a complete stop and they are told to exit by the driver.

answers.text: Position yourself outside of the bus to supervise unloading.
Put the transmission in neutral gear and put on the brakes.

explanation: All students should remain seated until the bus has come to a complete stop and they are told to exit by the driver.

question: Which statement is true of an Antilock Brake System?

answers.text: Boosts braking power at the brake shoes or pads.
By preventing wheel lockup during emergency braking, the driver can continue to steer and control the bus.
answers.explanation: An antilock brake system prevents wheels from locking up during emergency braking. Since the wheels do not lock up, the driver retains the ability to steer and control the bus.

answers.text: The ABS will decrease braking distance in an emergency.

explanation: An antilock brake system prevents wheels from locking up during emergency braking. Since the wheels do not lock up, the driver retains the ability to steer and control the bus.

question: An assertive bus driver is a driver who is:

answers.text: confident
answers.explanation: An assertive bus driver is confident in their abilities. They are effective communicators and do well responding with planned actions. They take charge of a situation and understand the needs of the students under their care.

answers.text: aggressive
passive

explanation: An assertive bus driver is confident in their abilities. They are effective communicators and do well responding with planned actions. They take charge of a situation and understand the needs of the students under their care.

question: Which of the following will result in a mandatory evacuation?

answers.text: A student has thrown a piece of paper in your direction.
There is an altercation between two students.
A hazardous materials spill.
answers.explanation: A hazardous materials spill will result in a mandatory evacuation of the school bus.

explanation: A hazardous materials spill will result in a mandatory evacuation of the school bus.

question: If the gate comes down after you have started to cross the railroad crossing you should:

answers.text: Drive through the gate.
answers.explanation: Should a railroad crossing gate begin to come down after you have started to cross the tracks, proceed through the crossing since a train is likely approaching, even if it means breaking the gate.

answers.text: Back up until you are completely off the tracks.
Stop immediately and call your dispatcher.

explanation: Should a railroad crossing gate begin to come down after you have started to cross the tracks, proceed through the crossing since a train is likely approaching, even if it means breaking the gate.

question: The zone that extends as much as 30 feet from the front bumper, 10 feet from the left and right sides of the bus and 10 feet behind the rear bumper of the school bus is called what?

answers.text: Danger Zone.
answers.explanation: Danger zones are areas where children are at risk of being struck by traffic during unloading and loading. Generally, these areas extend to 10 feet of either side of the bus, 10 feet behind the rear bumper, and 30 feet in front of the front bumper.

answers.text: Perimeter Zone.
Safety Zone.

explanation: Danger zones are areas where children are at risk of being struck by traffic during unloading and loading. Generally, these areas extend to 10 feet of either side of the bus, 10 feet behind the rear bumper, and 30 feet in front of the front bumper.

question: If students must cross the street after unloading off the bus, which statement is true?

answers.text: If the bus is equipped with the crossing gate, the children will not be in any danger.
When the driver signals that it is safe to proceed, the students must run across the street.
Crossing students should make eye contact with the bus driver and wait for the driver's signal that it is safe to proceed.
answers.explanation: Once the students have unloaded and are about 10 feet from the bus, they need to make eye contact with the driver and wait for the signal that it is safe to cross the road.

explanation: Once the students have unloaded and are about 10 feet from the bus, they need to make eye contact with the driver and wait for the signal that it is safe to cross the road.

question: Which tasks should be completed while approaching a school bus stop?

answers.text: Activate the flashing amber lights to warn other drivers
answers.explanation: The flashing amber lights should be activated about 200 feet (5 to 10 seconds) prior to reaching the bus stop. As you approach, monitor the mirrors for pedestrians, vehicles or other objects.

answers.text: Extend the STOP arm
Activate the red four-way flashers

explanation: The flashing amber lights should be activated about 200 feet (5 to 10 seconds) prior to reaching the bus stop. As you approach, monitor the mirrors for pedestrians, vehicles or other objects.

question: Backing a school bus is:

answers.text: Illegal in most states.
Strongly discouraged.
answers.explanation: Backing a school bus should be a last resort and only done when absolutely necessary.

answers.text: Fine, if you have a helper.

explanation: Backing a school bus should be a last resort and only done when absolutely necessary.

question: What viewing angle is provided by the overhead inside the rear-view mirror?

answers.text: Monitor the area behind the rear emergency exit door.
Monitor passenger activity along the inside of the bus.
answers.explanation: The overhead inside the rear-view mirror allows the bus driver to monitor passenger activity within the bus.

answers.text: Monitor the right side of the bus for approaching students.

explanation: The overhead inside the rear-view mirror allows the bus driver to monitor passenger activity within the bus.

question: The presence of an Antilock Braking System (ABS) can be easily identified by:

answers.text: A yellow ABS indicator lamp in the instrument panel when the ignition is placed in Run/Start
answers.explanation: Placing the ignition in the Run/Start position will begin a bulb prove-out (self-diagnostic) of the various warning indicators in the instrument panel. Vehicles with ABS will have a yellow/amber "ABS" lamp illuminate during the prove-out and then turn off. If the ABS light does not turn off during the prove-out, there is an issue that requires service.

answers.text: A yellow light on the left front corner of the bus.
Red letters spelling "ABS" on the brake pedal.

explanation: Placing the ignition in the Run/Start position will begin a bulb prove-out (self-diagnostic) of the various warning indicators in the instrument panel. Vehicles with ABS will have a yellow/amber "ABS" lamp illuminate during the prove-out and then turn off. If the ABS light does not turn off during the prove-out, there is an issue that requires service.

question: During an evacuation, a safe place for the students will be at least ____ feet off the road in the direction of oncoming traffic.

answers.explanation: During an evacuation, students should be placed at least 100 feet off the roadway in the direction of oncoming traffic.

explanation: During an evacuation, students should be placed at least 100 feet off the roadway in the direction of oncoming traffic.

question: The bus is trapped on the tracks, you should:

answers.text: Keep students calm and call your dispatcher.
Call 911 and wait for instructions.
Get everyone out of the bus and off the tracks immediately.
answers.explanation: If, for any reason, the school bus becomes stuck or stalled out on the tracks of a railroad crossing immediately evacuate the bus

explanation: If, for any reason, the school bus becomes stuck or stalled out on the tracks of a railroad crossing immediately evacuate the bus

question: Which task is part of the post-trip inspection of the school bus?

answers.text: Identifying any damage or vandalism
answers.explanation: The post-trip inspection needs to include checking for any sleeping students and if anyone left personal property. Looking for damage or vandalism and any mechanical or operational issues.

answers.text: The cleanliness of the seats and floors
That the waste basket is emptied after each trip

explanation: The post-trip inspection needs to include checking for any sleeping students and if anyone left personal property. Looking for damage or vandalism and any mechanical or operational issues.

question: All interior and exterior mirrors must be adjusted correctly to ensure:

answers.text: Blind spots around the bus can be monitored.
Danger zones around the bus can be monitored.
answers.explanation: The danger zone is the area on all sides of the bus where children are in the most danger of being hit, either by another vehicle or their own bus. Proper adjustment of mirrors ensures that the danger zones can be monitored by the driver.

answers.text: Every student can be seen from the driver's seat.

explanation: The danger zone is the area on all sides of the bus where children are in the most danger of being hit, either by another vehicle or their own bus. Proper adjustment of mirrors ensures that the danger zones can be monitored by the driver.

question: What will be the results of a failed antilock brake system on a bus?

answers.text: The transmission will shift to neutral and not allow you to drive forward.
ABS will no longer prevent wheel lockup, but base braking will be unaffected.
answers.explanation: It is not uncommon for one of the ABS sensors to fail at a wheel. A vehicle with a failed ABS will retain normal braking, but the wheels can now lock up under hard braking conditions. The yellow ABS light should be illuminated on the instrument panel. Refer the bus for service when returning to the bus garage after the route.

answers.text: The brake pedal will become very hard and require the driver to press harder to brake.

explanation: It is not uncommon for one of the ABS sensors to fail at a wheel. A vehicle with a failed ABS will retain normal braking, but the wheels can now lock up under hard braking conditions. The yellow ABS light should be illuminated on the instrument panel. Refer the bus for service when returning to the bus garage after the route.

question: Rear tail swing of a school bus is approximately:

answers.text: 8 feet
5 feet
3 feet
answers.explanation: Tail swing occurs when the movement of the rear portion of the vehicle swings in the opposite direction of the front end during a turn. Tail swing is determined by the length of the bus behind the rear axle and is usually about 3 feet of swing.

explanation: Tail swing occurs when the movement of the rear portion of the vehicle swings in the opposite direction of the front end during a turn. Tail swing is determined by the length of the bus behind the rear axle and is usually about 3 feet of swing.

question: Which area should be viewable when the left and right-side crossover mirrors are adjusted correctly?

answers.text: Both front tires touching the ground.
answers.explanation: When these two mirrors are properly adjusted the driver should be able to view both front tires touching the ground from the driver's seat.

answers.text: The full length of each side of the bus
Traffic in the left lane, and 10 feet away from the road where students will approach on the right

explanation: When these two mirrors are properly adjusted the driver should be able to view both front tires touching the ground from the driver's seat.

question: How often are most school bus drivers required to take a physical exam, including a vision test?

answers.text: Annually
Every two years
answers.explanation: Most bus drivers are required to take an annual physical exam that must include a vision test.

answers.text: Whenever they renew their state CDL

explanation: Most bus drivers are required to take an annual physical exam that must include a vision test.

question: How far away from the bus/road should students be when unloading is complete so the driver can leave?

answers.text: About 5 feet from either side of the bus
at least 10 feet from the side of the bus
answers.explanation: Students should be at least 10 feet away from the side of the bus and visible to the driver for unloading to be complete. Students who have crossed the road should be at least 10 feet off the roadway.

answers.text: More than 15 feet from the bus

explanation: Students should be at least 10 feet away from the side of the bus and visible to the driver for unloading to be complete. Students who have crossed the road should be at least 10 feet off the roadway.

question: Which exam is NOT part of the process to becoming a school bus driver?

answers.text: Air brake emergencies
answers.explanation: The process to become a school bus driver includes knowledge tests for CDL general, bus knowledge, and passenger vehicle knowledge

answers.text: CDL general knowledge
School bus specific test

explanation: The process to become a school bus driver includes knowledge tests for CDL general, bus knowledge, and passenger vehicle knowledge

question: Which situation will require you to evacuate the entire school bus?

answers.text: When backing up near a steep drop off
The bus breaking down on, or adjacent to, railroad tracks
answers.explanation: Trains cannot stop in time to avoid an accident themselves. If the bus breaks down in the path of a potential train (on or adjacent to the track) immediately evacuate the bus and move everyone to a safe location.

answers.text: The bus breaking down along the side of a freeway

explanation: Trains cannot stop in time to avoid an accident themselves. If the bus breaks down in the path of a potential train (on or adjacent to the track) immediately evacuate the bus and move everyone to a safe location.

question: You should adjust the outside left and right-side flat mirrors to see:

answers.text: 200 feet behind the bus.
answers.explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet behind the bus, which is about four bus lengths.

answers.text: The entire area to the rear of the mirror along the side of the bus.
The rear right and left tires touching the ground.

explanation: Proper adjustment of the left and right flat mirrors will result in the driver having visibility for 200 feet behind the bus, which is about four bus lengths.

question: Which of the following is true of the convex, crossover, and flat mirrors?

answers.text: They should be checked every 10 seconds while driving
They should be checked every 30 seconds while driving
They should be checked in a logical sequence
answers.explanation: Checking each of the three mirrors in a logical sequence will ensure that you never miss one.

explanation: Checking each of the three mirrors in a logical sequence will ensure that you never miss one.

question: Which is a characteristic of an antilock brake system (ABS)?

answers.text: Braking power is boosted to stop the vehicle quicker.
ABS decreases vehicle stopping distance.
Wheels are prevented from locking up so the driver can retain steering control while braking.
answers.explanation: On a non-ABS vehicle, a driver would release the brakes when the wheels lock up so that wheel can turn, and the driver can then steer. Essentially, an antilock braking system does the same thing, but hundreds of times per second. This allows the wheels to continue to turn so the driver can retain steering control while the brakes are applied.

explanation: On a non-ABS vehicle, a driver would release the brakes when the wheels lock up so that wheel can turn, and the driver can then steer. Essentially, an antilock braking system does the same thing, but hundreds of times per second. This allows the wheels to continue to turn so the driver can retain steering control while the brakes are applied.

question: Which protocol is recommended when loading students at a bus stop?

answers.text: Take a head count of students before and after loading at each stop
answers.explanation: The recommended protocol is to count each student waiting at the bus stop before giving them the signal to board. Count them again as they pass you while boarding and then take steps to account for any missing students.

answers.text: Point to each student and load them one at a time
Have the loading students count themselves and tell you if someone is missing

explanation: The recommended protocol is to count each student waiting at the bus stop before giving them the signal to board. Count them again as they pass you while boarding and then take steps to account for any missing students.

question: What is the primary purpose for conducting an inspection of the bus?

answers.text: To ensure operational integrity
To ensure efficiency during the bus route
To ensure safety for all students in accordance with federal, state, and local laws
answers.explanation: The primary purpose of conducting an inspection is to ensure the safety of everyone on the bus through the entire route in accordance with federal, state, and local laws.

explanation: The primary purpose of conducting an inspection is to ensure the safety of everyone on the bus through the entire route in accordance with federal, state, and local laws.

question: According to the CDL manual, the final step when picking up students at the bus stop is:

answers.text: Make a final check to confirm all traffic stops before opening the bus door and signaling students to approach.
answers.explanation: The final step in safely loading students is to wait until all traffic has completely stopped. Then, open the bus door and signal the student(s) to load.

answers.text: Place transmission in park or neutral and set parking brake.
Open the service door just enough to activate the alternating red light when traffic is in a safe distance from the bus.

explanation: The final step in safely loading students is to wait until all traffic has completely stopped. Then, open the bus door and signal the student(s) to load.

question: Before crossing the roadway, students will wait for you to:

answers.text: Give the universal crossing signal.
answers.explanation: Students should wait for the bus at least 10 feet off the roadway. Once the bus and all traffic has stopped, they should look to the driver to give the universal crossing signal so they may begin to cross the road.

answers.text: Nod your head.
Wave when it is safe to cross.

explanation: Students should wait for the bus at least 10 feet off the roadway. Once the bus and all traffic has stopped, they should look to the driver to give the universal crossing signal so they may begin to cross the road.

question: How often must the majority of bus drivers have a physical examination?

answers.text: Every year
Every two years
A vision test annually and a physical every two years.
answers.explanation: To ensure safe job performance, drivers aged 65 and over must have a physical annually. All other drivers must have a physical exam (with a vision test) every two years.

explanation: To ensure safe job performance, drivers aged 65 and over must have a physical annually. All other drivers must have a physical exam (with a vision test) every two years.

question: Your bus is equipped with roof-mounted, white strobe lights. When should the overhead strobe lights be used?

answers.text: When you have limited visibility.
answers.explanation: Roof mounted strobe lights are activated during times of limited visibility to signal other drivers of your location, such as when it is foggy or raining heavily.

answers.text: When traffic is heavy.
At all times.

explanation: Roof mounted strobe lights are activated during times of limited visibility to signal other drivers of your location, such as when it is foggy or raining heavily.

question: What mirror is used to monitor passenger activity inside the bus?

answers.text: Overhead rearview mirror.
answers.explanation: The overhead mirror is mounted directly over the driver allowing for visibility of the passenger area to the rear of the bus.

answers.text: Side crossover mirrors.
Side convex mirrors.

explanation: The overhead mirror is mounted directly over the driver allowing for visibility of the passenger area to the rear of the bus.

question: While loading and unloading students you should check your mirrors:

answers.text: After entering traffic
After you close the door.
Continuously.
answers.explanation: During loading and unloading, continuously monitor mirrors to ensure that approaching traffic has stopped from the front and rear and that no other dangers are approaching while students are clearing the road.

explanation: During loading and unloading, continuously monitor mirrors to ensure that approaching traffic has stopped from the front and rear and that no other dangers are approaching while students are clearing the road.

question: Why should you be extra cautious at a passive crossing?

answers.text: This type of crossing does have any type of traffic control device and the decision to proceed rests entirely in your hands.
answers.explanation: An active crossing provides traffic control, such as a crossing arm and an alarm. A passive crossing does not have any form of traffic control device. Regardless, the procedure that a bus driver must follow at a crossing is the same for both active and passive.

answers.text: This type of crossing has a passing lane and you need to be alert for passing vehicles.
This type of crossing is used for crossing high speed train tracks.

explanation: An active crossing provides traffic control, such as a crossing arm and an alarm. A passive crossing does not have any form of traffic control device. Regardless, the procedure that a bus driver must follow at a crossing is the same for both active and passive.

question: If equipped, an inspection of a vehicle lift will include:

answers.text: the lift being stowed in the fully extended position.
checking that no parts are missing, leaking, or damaged.
answers.explanation: Inspection of a vehicle lift will include ensuring that no parts are missing, leaking, or damaged. The lift should also be fully retracted and latched securely.

answers.text: verifying that warning devices activate when the lift is in use.

explanation: Inspection of a vehicle lift will include ensuring that no parts are missing, leaking, or damaged. The lift should also be fully retracted and latched securely.

question: Under which situation would a bus driver avoid evacuating a bus?

answers.text: If the evacuation would expose students to significant outside dangers
answers.explanation: An evacuation should be avoided if injured students would be worse off by being moved, or outside conditions would expose students to significant dangers, so it is safer to stay on the bus.

answers.text: When the front half of a bus is stalled on railroad tracks
During the winter time when the temperature is below 35 degrees fahrenheit.

explanation: An evacuation should be avoided if injured students would be worse off by being moved, or outside conditions would expose students to significant dangers, so it is safer to stay on the bus.

question: When should you activate the right turn signal when approaching a school bus stop?

answers.text: Approximately 50 to 100 feet (1 to 3 seconds) prior to stopping
Approximately 100 to 300 feet (3 to 5 seconds) prior to stopping
answers.explanation: A driver needs to activate the right turn signal approximately 100 to 300 feet (3 to 5) prior to stopping at a bus stop.

answers.text: Approximately 200 to 400 feet (4 to 6 seconds) prior to stopping

explanation: A driver needs to activate the right turn signal approximately 100 to 300 feet (3 to 5) prior to stopping at a bus stop.

question: Is it ever acceptable to NOT report an accident to the school?

answers.text: No, all accidents must be reported no matter how minor.
answers.explanation: Regardless of damage, injury, or death, all school bus related accidents must be reported.

answers.text: Yes, only accidents resulting in property damage must be reported.
Yes, only accidents resulting in personal injury must be reported.

explanation: Regardless of damage, injury, or death, all school bus related accidents must be reported.

question: Which evacuation procedure protocol is true?

answers.text: If time allows, have all passengers exit from the front of the bus since it is safer to use the steps when unloading the bus rather than the emergency door.
If time allows, count all of the passengers before evacuating the bus and then take a headcount once you've reached a safe destination.
If time allows, contact dispatch to inform them of the situation, your location, and the assistance required before evacuating.
answers.explanation: When time allows, contact dispatch to inform them of the situation, provide your location, and explain what type of assistance you require.

explanation: When time allows, contact dispatch to inform them of the situation, provide your location, and explain what type of assistance you require.

question: All school buses are legally required to be which color?

answers.text: Banana Peel Yellow
Public School Amber
National School Bus Glossy Yellow
answers.explanation: NHTSA guidelines established the recommendation that school buses be painted National School Bus Glossy Yellow. All states now require this recommendation to be the color of all school buses.

explanation: NHTSA guidelines established the recommendation that school buses be painted National School Bus Glossy Yellow. All states now require this recommendation to be the color of all school buses.

question: What is the proper instruction that each student should receive regarding crossing the street when unloading the bus?

answers.text: Once the door opens students should quickly exit the bus and run/jog across the street to safety.
Students should exit the bus and assemble together where the driver can see them. Then, when given a signal by the driver, they should move across the street within the driver's view.
answers.explanation: Students should be instructed to exit the bus and assemble together where the driver can see them about 10 feet to the left front of the bus. Once they get the sign from the driver, they can walk across the street while remaining in view of the driver.

answers.text: Students should exit the bus and assemble together where the driver can see them. Once the bus leaves the students may disperse and cross the street.

explanation: Students should be instructed to exit the bus and assemble together where the driver can see them about 10 feet to the left front of the bus. Once they get the sign from the driver, they can walk across the street while remaining in view of the driver.

question: The left and right outside convex mirrors should be adjusted to allow visibility to:

answers.text: the left and right sides of the bus up to the mirror mounts.
answers.explanation: When properly adjusted the driver should have visibility to three areas: 1) the front portion of the rear tires touching the ground, 2) at least one lane on either side of the bus, and 3) the left and right sides of the bus up to the mirror mounts.

answers.text: the rear of the front tires touching the ground
the 12 feet danger zone in front of the bus.

explanation: When properly adjusted the driver should have visibility to three areas: 1) the front portion of the rear tires touching the ground, 2) at least one lane on either side of the bus, and 3) the left and right sides of the bus up to the mirror mounts.

question: In which situation would you recommend evacuating the bus?

answers.text: During a tornado watch.
A fire has started in the engine compartment.
answers.explanation: Essentially, any situation where remaining on the bus would result in greater danger than evacuating the bus.

answers.text: The bus has broken down and you are waiting for another bus.

explanation: Essentially, any situation where remaining on the bus would result in greater danger than evacuating the bus.

question: Which of the following is required to change the location of a school bus stop?

answers.text: Approval of the student(s)
Written approval of all parents who have children effected by the new stop
Written approval of the school district
answers.explanation: School districts set up official routes and bus stops before the start of the school year. Changing a bus stop will require written approval from the school district.

explanation: School districts set up official routes and bus stops before the start of the school year. Changing a bus stop will require written approval from the school district.

question: Who is primarily responsible for managing disciplinary issues on the bus during a route?

answers.text: The driver
answers.explanation: The driver is primarily responsible for managing any disciplinary issues on the bus during the route. Issues should be addressed with the bus stopped and neither loading nor unloading. If a student must be removed from the bus and you are not at the school or the student's designated stop, then call dispatch and request a school administrator or police officer to remove the student.

answers.text: The police
School administration

explanation: The driver is primarily responsible for managing any disciplinary issues on the bus during the route. Issues should be addressed with the bus stopped and neither loading nor unloading. If a student must be removed from the bus and you are not at the school or the student's designated stop, then call dispatch and request a school administrator or police officer to remove the student.

question: What is included in the inspection of a battery and battery box as part of your pre-trip vehicle inspection?

answers.text: Measuring battery voltage
The connections for the battery cables need to be secure and have minimal corrosion
answers.explanation: An inspection of the battery and battery box includes ensuring that both are secured. The battery caps should be in place. The connections for the battery cables need to be secure and have minimal corrosion. Measuring battery voltage would not be done unless there is a no-start issue and very few batteries today require distilled water to be added.

answers.text: Filling the battery with distilled water

explanation: An inspection of the battery and battery box includes ensuring that both are secured. The battery caps should be in place. The connections for the battery cables need to be secure and have minimal corrosion. Measuring battery voltage would not be done unless there is a no-start issue and very few batteries today require distilled water to be added.

question: While loading, it you cannot account for a student outside you should

answers.text: Secure the bus, turn off the engine, take the key, set the brake and check around and underneath the bus.
answers.explanation: If the driver cannot account for a student outside of the bus, the protocol is to turn off the engine, secure the bus, take the keys, set the brake, and search for the student around or underneath the bus.

answers.text: Continue your route, you may be penalized for being late.
Call your dispatcher.

explanation: If the driver cannot account for a student outside of the bus, the protocol is to turn off the engine, secure the bus, take the keys, set the brake, and search for the student around or underneath the bus.

question: More students are killed while ____ each year than are killed as passengers inside of a school bus.

answers.text: running towards the bus
crossing the street
getting on or off a school bus
answers.explanation: It is much more dangerous outside of the bus than it is inside the bus. More children are killed during the loading and unloading process than they are as passengers riding back and forth to school.

explanation: It is much more dangerous outside of the bus than it is inside the bus. More children are killed during the loading and unloading process than they are as passengers riding back and forth to school.

question: What is the appropriate response to behavioral issues that occur while you are driving the bus?

answers.text: Stop and secure the bus, then escort the student off the bus
Yell at the student to straighten up while viewing them in the overhead mirror
Stop, secure the bus, and then speak with the student about the issue.
answers.explanation: Do not take your eyes away from the road to confront the student, especially when loading or unloading. Stop and secure the bus, then stand up and walk to the student to speak to them in a courteous manner to address and resolve the issue.

explanation: Do not take your eyes away from the road to confront the student, especially when loading or unloading. Stop and secure the bus, then stand up and walk to the student to speak to them in a courteous manner to address and resolve the issue.

question: Which should be considered when driving a school bus in high winds?

answers.text: Look for large trucks or other obstacles to block the wind
Grip the steering wheel with both hands and prepare for buffeting
answers.explanation: The large flat sides of a school bus present a significant area for the wind to push against. Buffeting can change driving characteristics and cause the bus to try to change direction. Slow down from your normal speed and grasp the steering wheel with both hands to retain control when winds blow against the bus.

answers.text: Increase speed to push more mass against the wind to combat buffeting

explanation: The large flat sides of a school bus present a significant area for the wind to push against. Buffeting can change driving characteristics and cause the bus to try to change direction. Slow down from your normal speed and grasp the steering wheel with both hands to retain control when winds blow against the bus.

question: How far away from the nearest rail should you stop a highway rail-crossing?

answers.text: Stop no closer than 15 feet and no farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail, where you have the best view of the tracks.
answers.explanation: As a bus driver you need to stop within 15 and 50 feet of the nearest rail, seeking the optimum view of the tracks.

answers.text: Stop no closer than 5 feet and no farther than 25 feet from the nearest rail, where you have the best view of the tracks.
Stop no closer than 20 feet and no farther than 30 feet from the nearest rail, where you have the best view of the tracks.

explanation: As a bus driver you need to stop within 15 and 50 feet of the nearest rail, seeking the optimum view of the tracks.
